nodes.label: Tests for the built-in working-directory / worktree policy (:mod:`omnigent.polic
Reduce a policy result to its decision string for terse assertions.      :param
With no allowed_dirs, every cd-family command is denied (the core guard).      A
A cd into an allowed directory abstains (None), so the agent can use it.
A cd into a subdirectory of an allowed dir abstains.      Proves prefix matching
A cd outside the allowed dirs is denied even when allowed_dirs is set.      If t
``/workspace-evil`` must NOT match allowed ``/workspace`` as a subdir.      The
``popd`` (no determinable target) is denied even when allowed_dirs is set.
``cd -P /workspace`` resolves its target past the ``-P`` flag.      The flag mus
``git -C /other status`` is gated as a directory switch under block_cd.
Plain local git commands (no -C, no worktree switch) abstain.      Gating ``git
``git worktree add/move/remove`` are denied — they switch worktrees.
Read/maintenance worktree subcommands abstain — they don't switch trees.      De
A git global option that takes a value doesn't hide the worktree subcmd.      ``
With block_cd=False: cd abstains, but worktree switches are still gated.      Pr
``action="ask"`` parks a gated command for approval instead of denying.
``ls && cd /etc`` denies on the cd (most-restrictive composition).      Proves s
A single ``&`` (background operator) is a command separator too.      Without sp
``bash -c`` / ``sh -c`` / ``eval`` wrappers are unwrapped and gated.      Withou
A leading env-assignment / ``sudo`` prefix doesn't hide the cd.      ``FOO=bar c
A leading subshell ``(`` doesn't disguise the cd.      ``(cd /etc && ls)`` token
An un-tokenizable segment that looks like a cd is gated, not allowed.      Unbal
Commands that touch no directory/worktree abstain, so the policy composes.
A non-shell tool call is abstained on (not this policy's surface).
A custom shell tool name is parsed when listed in ``shell_tools``.      With ``s
The default ``shell_tools`` set includes Claude/Codex native ``Bash``.      If t
An unknown ``action`` fails loud at build time, not silently at runtime.
Disabling both gates fails loud — the policy could never fire otherwise.
block_working_dir_changes resolves and a cd DENYs through the engine.
An ``action=ask`` worktree switch surfaces as ASK through the engine.      Prove
block_working_dir_changes is discovered as a factory entry with a schema.      F
The schema accepts valid params and rejects unknown keys / wrong types.
